channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Jannies, excuse me while I KILL MYSELF — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Jannies, excuse me while I KILL MYSELF

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-27 23:52 UTC

OP

It's fucked for real this time. completely dead. AI bubble will pop, S&P will collapse and drag BTC even lower, at a certain point Saylor will get liquidated. Tom lee will get liquidated. BTC at $15k and ETH at $200 is not unrealistic. Short all crypto. I've been in this space for a decade. It's truly dead. Saylor will be sucking cock in hell wondering where it all went wrong. Tom Lee will shove a pineapple up his own ass. We are FUCKED. IT IS FUCKING OVER. CRYPTO IS DEAD. THE CLARITY ACT WILL NEVER PASS. BLACKROCK WILL DIVEST. BINANCE WILL SHUT DOWN. WE ARE ALL FUCKED AND GOING TO SUCK COCK IN HELL. VITALIK WILL BE PERFORMING FELATIO ON ORTHODOX JEWISH RABBI IN HELL. WE BOUGHT A FUCKING PET ROCK FAKE PIECE OF FAGGOT SHIT ABANDON SHIP NOW NOW !!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62281675
whoopsie poopsie xD

62281676
????????????

62281679
Better luck next time

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281685
>>62281679
Oh my FUCKING GOD

62281691
JUST IN TIME FOR CHAINLINKS 7 YEAR ANIVERSARY ON MAINNET AND THE RELEASE OF INEFERNO TO BOOT LMAOOO

62281698
>>62281668
Should've bought HBAR...!

62281705
>pink ID

Yeah you’re fucked buddy

62281711
Literal bottom signal

62281713
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6bghDcbzfEU

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=74NluS3jzTo

brb meds

62281742
OP here. I'm back. I'm more relaxed and thinking logically now. Realistically they are going to begin having military style tribunals for crypto advocates. Arthur hayes will be buck broken on CSPAN. Tom lee will shove a pineapple up his own ass. Satoshi nakatoto will be finally revealed and be drawn.and quartered on national television (in israel for some reason) we are so fucked. It is so over. (Not reading replies)

62281754
>>62281668
ive been in crypto since 2016

you are an emotional pussy nothing will happen or ever happen stfu

62281776
>>62281754
Fuck you homo. Tom Lee pineapple prepping faggot

62281782
>>62281776
you said you weren't reading replies!

62281789
>>62281668
>ai bubble pops
>new tech fad emerges
>tech giants become even more profitable than before
>spx goes up nonstop forever
>crypto remains a pump and dump scamcoin forever

62281798
>>62281776
Your father is a FAGGOT and your mother FUCKS NIGGERS

62281831
>>62281798
Alright this is true

62281862
>>62281742
no crying in the nigger casino

62281983
>>62281776
no fuck you nigger god u guys just cry nonstop fuck u fuck u

item.title: NOT A DRILL — 18 replies — 37.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NOT A DRILL

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-27 23:29 UTC

OP

YouTube is demonetizing automated channels left and right. Youtube automation was supposed to be a stable source of passive income and now people are losing their empires. Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?

Early replies (first 3)

62277230
>>62277227
Cry me a river, dickface.

62277233
>>62277227
Saar...

62277354
>>62277227
>Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?
They needed people to upload AI slop for that data to be fed into the algorithms to train them to punish that content.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278141
>>62277227
VLC should sue.

62278168
>>62277227
>you can't make money by showing people content they like because you made it with an easy to use tool that produces good content that people like!!! okay???
shorting goog tomorrow

62278201
>>62277227
Content creation is on the chopping block to be automated. People and agents will do it for free. No more payouts to content creators. More profit for GOOG. It’s pretty obvious, really.

62278236
>>62278168
Nobody likes AI slop, AI schizo. Also, not buying your AI bags, you're on your own.

62278254
>>62278236
"Nobody wants a car" said the horse

62278401
>>62278254
I want to watch actual videos of whores and cats, not fake slop created by jeets.

62278404
>>62277227
get a real job

62278414
What about the AI ads?

62278783
>>62278401
AI Cat weapon doorcam videos are better than any real cat video ever made.

62280180
>>62278404
Being an influencer is a real job

62280218
>>62277227
>inauthentic content
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH
Got 'em!

As someone who makes 100% original videos for fun, this makes me happy. I don't make money, I do get a decent number of views. perhaps this will change things.
I ran some youtube theory through a few AI models. AI predicts more wholesome unpolished "real" content will get popular. This try hard, insincerity "Youtuber" "influencer" model is fading.

62280279
Huge news for India

62280285
thank god. I kept getting recommended this climbing channel that would document 100% hallucinated climbing disasters as if they really happened. all of the stories were framed as anti-western morality tales. probably prompted by a resentful jeet.

62280342
>Nooooo now I have to do my own voiceovers on my YouTube videos instead of using AI TTS and nobody is going to listen anymore once they hear my Indian accent nooooooooo

62281915
>>62277227
Good, I'm getting sick of blocking AI and slop channels.

item.title: Get the fuck back to work and pump my folio wage bitch! — 7 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Get the fuck back to work and pump my folio wage bitch!

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-27 22:56 UTC

OP

Those dividends aren't going to make their way to my account themselves without your hard work......
How do you respond without sounding mad? Today I woke up at 10 AM (no alarm) looked at the clock and just thought "KEK Wagies" I don't understand why anyone still wages if they have 500k+.... Whats your excuse anon?

>Mortgage?
Wage Prism

>Wife and kids
Lol LMAO

>Career
ZOG daycare

My heart goes out to the trad cucks out there who genuinely believe in the whole 2.5 kids and white picket fence thing, what keeps you waging /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

62275963
incel thread

62277455
>>62275712
I first need to get to 500k. I can feel my spirit sinking everyday..another day, another dollar right?

62278449
Fresh NEET here. The first thing I have done after becoming NEET two weeks ago was to get drunk on monday at 7:00 AM on bus station to spite wagies their workweek start.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278520
Yeah, most people are born to work and 2026 is absolutely terrible to be working class. The ladder has been pulled up.
0 wage growth in first world countries since 2020. Making the average salary means you’re falling behind.

62278529
Schd and amplify give me bigger raises than my company every year

62278728
>>62275712
500k wouldn't even get me halfway through a small house or apartment here. What would I do with it so I don't need to work?

62281832
>>62278728
Nothing. Keep working. Lol.
Modern working class life is so pointless.

item.title: Rescue your dying value — 4 replies — 4.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Rescue your dying value

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-27 23:04 UTC

OP

Swap your junk for XMR, purposeless coins all going to 0 before Jan 1 2028

Early replies (first 3)

62280927
>>62280863
yeah im good.

i like XMR but the community is giga cringe anytime you mention anything about price.

ETH is going to valhalla

62281189
>>62280927
absolute knuckle dragging retard right here

62281362
>>62280863
WHAT IS THIS KUMAR SHILL KEK? Its okay form a fucking shitnigger icp or sol holder, but u pedofags too? u guys r cheap nowadays...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281860
>>62280927
>i like XMR but the community is giga cringe anytime you cry about not making gorillions off the delisted DNM money
ftfy

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 99 replies — 66.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 99 • Updated: 2026-05-27 22:28 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278664
>>62278661
*shiny syndrome

62278665
>>62278661
>zero arguments
I accept your concession.

62278671
>>62278665
what concession? it's you stupid nigger that is BEGGING for attention like you're going to get beaten to a pulp by your handlers if you dont spam your dead tech enough
>Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.
>Yes it is in every thread right now becase I am posting about it nigger, because I hold LINK and find it strange how everyone is just fucking ignoring it ok? How about you discuss the tech instead of making up stories about hedera shills.
>wordswordswords yet you said nothing regarding CLPR, only adhoms directed at me, because you did zero fucking research on this CLPR thing and are just complacent that whatever this new tech is it won't kill CCIP.
lol. lmao even

62278679
>>62278671
Still not an argument.

62278690
>>62278660
That is not what I said. The anon you replied to was right, direct ledger-to-ledger is impossible, it's still a bridge like any other, simply because the way the internet works. Off-chain simply means outside the execution environment of the blockchain. RPC nodes still absolutely communicate through the interent like any traffic people generate from their computers. It's not even a jab at hbar, direct ledger-to-ledger is just a marketing term. CCIP works the same way, so the question is what is so revolutionary about CLPR?

62278702
>>62275877
chainlink has more features than tech now. use the chainlink standard to lower enterprise risk

62278704
>>62278690
>it's still a bridge like any other
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
>For this reason, Hashgraph developed CLPR (pronounced “Clipper”), a new BRIDGELESS Cross-Ledger Protocol that takes a fundamentally different approach. Rather than pooling locked assets or delegating trust to a set of permissioned validators, CLPR uses cryptographic state proofs to let tokens, data, and arbitrary messages move between ledgers directly, with no custodian, no pooled liquidity, and no single point of failure.
How CLPR works:
>In Step 1, each ledger runs CLPR endpoints: dedicated servers that communicate directly with their counterparts on the other ledger. These endpoints exchange proofs to verify and settle cross-ledger transfers of messages and tokens. At no point does any intermediary hold or control assets. If an endpoint goes offline, transfers pause and resume automatically when connectivity is restored. Funds are never at risk.
>In Step 2, once both ledgers have implemented CLPR natively, the validators themselves become the endpoints. No separate infrastructure is required. Each ledger verifies the other’s state using its own native proof mechanism, with no additional layer in between.

62278707
How many chainlink killers are we at right now?

62278710
>>62278707
This is not a Chainlink killer, it's just a CCIP killer.

62278726
>>62278704
this doesn't have the security standards of chainlink, it could be better than layerzero.

62278731
>>62278726
>cryptographic state proofs
the setup on both chains is difficult when using proofs
>CLPR’s initial deployment is interoperability between HashSphere private enterprise networks and the Hedera public network, covering both Sphere-to-Sphere and Sphere-to-Hedera transfers.
they have this integrated on their own chains, but don't have it on other blockchains that run in different environments

62278842
another day, another reason that LINK SUPREMACY is inevitable

62278958
>>62278704
>"WE DON'T USE BRIDGES OR ORACLES TO PUT EXTERNAL DATA ON CHAIN"
>uses "dedicated servers" (step 1) or "the validators themselves" (step 2) to put external data on chain
In other words it uses oracles to put external data on chain.

Also in step 2 other chains have to write CLPR into their node code.
Good luck with that.

62280692
>>62278958
>In other words it uses oracles to put external data on chain.
They never claim not to use oracles onchain, but they don't use oracles offchain so there's no bridge. It's bridgless unlike CCIP there's no third party needed.

62280745
>>62280692
>there's no third party needed
In step 1 the "dedicated servers" are third parties.
In step 2 the chain validators are supposed to moonlight as oracles. Which is retarded in itself, but that's not the worst part; because step 2 implies fundamentally changing how chain miners/validators/nodes/... work to include CLPR functionality.

62280757
>>62280745
>In step 1 the "dedicated servers" are third parties.
No they aren't. Because they are ran in each ledger. There are then only two parties. One in one ledger and the other in the other ledger. So only two parties. No third party middle-man bridge needed like CCIP.

62280772
>>62280757
CLPR:
>sender
>server on Chain A
>server on Chain B
>receiver

CCIP:
>sender
>CCIP
>receiver

hmmmmmmmm

62280805
>>62280772
CLPR:
>source ledger
>destination ledger

CCIP:
>source ledger
>router
>committing DON
>executing DON
>router
>destination ledger

62280841
>>62280805
The router is a contract you dumbass. Not a "party". CLPR uses smart contracts too.

And without fundamentally changing every chain in existence to include CLPR in their base node code, the comparison boils down to this:

CCIP:
>source ledger
>committing DON
>executing DON
>destination ledger

CLPR:
>source ledger
>dedicated server on the source ledger
>dedicated server on the destination ledger
>destination ledger

62280984
>>62280841
Right so we can argue semantics all day but the thing is that with CLPR you don't need an offchain bridge, the ledgers talk directly with each other, that's the point. This is interesting because with CCIP you have to pay a fee to Sergey each time you use it but with CLPR you don't really have to pay anyone. Users will most likely use the cheaper option. And unlike all the bridge hacks there's nothing to hack here because it's not a bridge.

62281003
>>62280984
>the ledgers talk directly with each other
Only if those leddgers fundamentally changed their source codes to include CLPR, and the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... pull double duty as oracles too.

In the absence of that, the ledgers talk to each other via intermediaries called "dedicated servers".

62281021
>>62281003
>the ledgers talk to each other via intermediaries called "dedicated servers".
The CLPR endpoints are not intermediares because they are "inside" each ledger so to speak. It's not a bridge. It's never offchain.
>These endpoints exchange proofs to verify and settle cross-ledger transfers of messages and tokens. At no point does any intermediary hold or control assets. If an endpoint goes offline, transfers pause and resume automatically when connectivity is restored. Funds are never at risk.

62281047
>>62281021
>The CLPR endpoints are not intermediares because they are "inside" each ledger so to speak.
No they're not.
That would only happen when "once both ledgers have implemented CLPR natively", and "the validators themselves become the endpoints".
i.e. after the ledgers in question fundamentally changed their source codes to include CLPR, and the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... pull double duty as oracles too.

62281161
>>62281047
Btw why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? Honestly seems like the ideal solution and far better than CCIP or any other bridge. Is it that you just think devs will be too lazy to change shit?

62281185
>>62281161
>why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level?
The fact that every chain has to alter their base code to include CLPR functionality.

62281195
>>62281185
Yes. There is effort to be done. There is also effort to be done when a chain pays CLL to integrate CCIP. Why are those different? It's all just paying with either time or money to integrate with either CCIP or CLPR.

62281245
>>62281195
No chain is going to fundamentally change how they work for Hedera.
And Chainlink also works with cryptographic proofs end to end. Zero need to make L1 miners/validators/nodes/... pull double duty as oracles.

One of the oldest Chainlink fuds was "Ethereum will have native oracles". This CLPR shit is an even more retarded version of that.

62281268
>>62281245
This is not a Hedera thing.
Ethereum can talk with Solana with CLPR.

62281635
>>62275877
Not another LINK killer. It's over for like the 30th time

62281760
>>62275877
all the blocking they are doing shows how scared they are to lose retail funding. we need this token to crash to zero so the last 300m coins they have are worthless. this will light a fire under sergey azz. bankrupt them faster. force layoffs.

item.title: DAVE RAMSEY SAYS BUY A PRESSURE WASHER — 19 replies — 38.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
DAVE RAMSEY SAYS BUY A PRESSURE WASHER

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-27 23:28 UTC

OP

>"You don't want to be 63 years old still pressure washing. But to get through this week, you can do a lot of pressure washing"

If you cant get a job or get hired go get a pressure washer and start going door to door offering your service.

Early replies (first 3)

62281547
NIGGER STOP MAKING THIS THREAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAD

62281548
>>62281541
the demand for pressure washing is fr crazy no cap

62281603
he should stick to cooking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281605
>>62281541
if i see this nigga on the street im gonna scream nigger in his face

62281611
how many $600 wash jobs has ramsey the gaslighter done this week? boomers really are fucking stupid

62281671
>>62281541
My mom bought me a pressure washer and a van for my birthday.

62281695
Bros csn I go to a massage parlor and request a $600 pressure washer service?

62281737
>>62281541
>code school
Is this meme still going around boomer circles?

>pressure washing
What about when the economy is so shit no one wants to pay for a superficial service like pressure washing?

62281756
>>62281541

Met a guy who owned a pressure washer business. On the cheap end start up cost is $25,000. That’s just the industrial size washer. Retard boomer thinks you can wash concrete at an entire strip mall with a house use pressure washer.

Boomers are really fucken retarded and so is anyone else who listens to Ramsey. Someone post the fees on the garbage funds he shills

62281763
>>62281541
>Coding

62281767
>>62281737

>boomer mentions coding when it’s already done by underpaid jeets and AI

Holy shit

62281773
>>62281541
Why would anyone pay $600 for a pressure wash job when you could literally buy your own pressure washer for a fraction of the cost.

62281777
>>62281737
>What about when the economy is so shit no one wants to pay for a superficial service like pressure washing?
That’s when wash jobs turn into blow jobs.

62281808
>>62281541
Who the hell is paying $600 for a power washing?

62281815
>>62281808
>Who the hell is paying $600 for a power washing?
businesses and governments mostly.

4chan can't imagine selling something to a business or the government. It simply doesn't cross their minds. Even though businesses and governments have about a million times more money to waste than anyone here.

62281827
>>62281815

Honestly window cleaning seems cheaper and a faster income source if you do it for small businesses

62281830
>>62281827
it is.

neither one is very difficult though.

62281902
>>62281541
>start noticing the Dave Ramsey pressure washer meme catching on
>dad ordered a pressure washer from Home Depot and make me pick it up a few days ago
What did the Universe mean by this?

62281913
>>62281541
I just farted. How much can I get?

item.title: why does this keep happening — 167 replies — 28.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 167 • Updated: 2026-05-27 18:05 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271096
>>62271088
>>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
The ENTIRE 2022-2023 (((range))) was ENTIRELY due to BTC dump-based LINK suppression keeping the price in that range.

62271099
>>62271074
Not the gotcha you believe this is. Another lesson from trading stocks. The same people tend to trade and hold the same stocks over and over. What this means is every chart has a temperament to it. Like how there are cats, but not all cats are the same. Got it? Different temperament. So the link chart, from what you all continually show, is that links temperament is for consistent flat resistance levels. This probably won't change for the link until enough new investors/traders join the morphus blob of temperament to change it. Which is good because it lends some credibility to link repeating the same patterns so you can profit from them.

62271104
>>62271099
>Why none from the 2017 era?
>Gets shown one from the 2017 era
>Instant wall of pilpul
hahahaha

62271106
>>62271096
lel I was just about to post this.
This faggot is as blind and clueless as he is wordy and spammy.

And now he's going all new age on us:
>>62271099
>What this means is every chart has a temperament to it.

Dude's about to break out the mood crystals.

62271116
>>62271020
>And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters
you clearly do, which is why you are here calling people "schizos" for noticing BTC dumps whenever LINK gets positive news (pic rel). i dont spend a single minute discussing people in xrp threads or twitter: why would you post 26 times in this thread?

you have done this before havent you

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

Please shut the fuck up. You are gay. Muuh spirituals.

62277201
>>62273954
You're theories only make sense to 4chan posters with a persecution kink. Bring them outside this site and you're into heavy schizo territory.

62277225
>>62277201
Fallacy ad populum, faggot. You argue like a woman

62277275
Its weird checking in on this board like once a week and seeing the usual paid fud just never stops, and how much of it just never gets responded to or probably even seen
There's at least like 10-20k hours worth of collective fuddie manpower that would have made no difference if it had never existed at all. It would be tragic if the time of third world subhumans was worth anything to begin with

62277295
>>62277225
>the stuff we say is true because we can bias it into reality y...you're just wrong for disagreeing with our spam
Half this thread is top of the debate pyramid calling me retarded every other post. You faggots wonder why ironic shit posting is all that happens here.

62277481
>>62271176
You are Jewish as fuck.

62277668
>>62263230
Imagine telling people in 2019 that in 2026 people would be theorizing that LINK is being suppressed under $10 KEK

It was supposed to be $1000 by now

62277674
>>62271202
Bearish article paid for by link whales hunting for exit liquidity

62277803
>>62277295
>30pbtid

62278136
>>62277295
You tried to double down despite another anon posting like a dozen screencaps proving that link is specifically targeted by suppression, which is just a fraction of how many times it has actually happened. Link absolutely 100 percent is a suppressed asset
So yes. You are a retard. A useless, shit-eating brown retard who sucks at his "job" trying to gaslight people on a dead board.

62278171
you link fags have ruined this entire board with your schitzo nonsense. this is proof to me that having higher-than-average iq doesnt necessarily mean that you are 'smart' if you cant understand the greater context of why your coin is failing in the first place

62278428
>>62278171
>schitzo nonsense
This thread is full of objective, verifiable proof. It cannot be denied.
At the most you can claim that this is not exceptional and the same happens to all alts, but so far nobody has ever shown a shred of proof after years of making exactly this claim.

It's schizo to deny this is happening.

62279583
>>62277275
>fud
why do cuckolds use this word? Just check the chart

62279597
Looks like LINK suppression finally killed BTC

I wonder whats gonna happen next

62279623
>>62279597
mhh....your cuckold token crashes to $2 and misses another bullrun? Just a guess

62280508
>>62277275
Remember the Windows VR cooking spirit advertising with a VR world full of corpses?
https://www.breitbart.com/tech/2020/04/14/microsoft-publishes-then-takes-down-commercial-with-spirit-cooker-marina-abramovic/

Windows served as an intermediary in spiritual attacks during my sleep and I had to switch to linux to stop them.

I am starting to think that Chainlink becoming integrated into the financial world into every transaction would conquer darkness and insert light into every transaction.

>>62271066
How does it feel to serve evil and to deny light and god?
The result is always the same because darkness cannot exist without light as they are energy vampires.
So yes this planet can be entirely corrupted and light can leave it and everything will be saved that way but even you won't enjoy that experience because you will be their next sacrifice when they run out of babies to torture.

62280516
>>62264135
>buy into your secret club we are massively advertising
kek

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 44 replies — 26.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-05-27 22:17 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273163
>>62273152
Well I haven't graduated yet, but employment data for my school is really good. It all depends on where you go and how well you do it seems.
>>62273154
It will skyrocket from $9.44 to $9.45

62273171
does it seem like most of your classmates are smart? I was at a high school reunion recently and spoke to a guy who loafed around doing menial work for like 15 years after college. then he took the lsat and got into Northwestern. he has a big time job now defending people in trouble with the SEC.

62273174
>>62273171
Yeah, how many law students are just dumbfucks?

62273234
Junior lawyer is literally technician in legal system. Very far from management and get treated like disposable human trash

62273383
>>62272812
be like this guy anon
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G-6eynsaZ0U&t=836s

62273401
>>62272812
Should I become a lawyer? My college is paid for and I feel like it'll be easy with AI now

62273402
>>62273041
The Irish are better writers the British

62273648
>>62273171
>>62273174
Nobody I'd call dumb but some came across as pretty lazy. Also AI use is absurd.
>>62273401
It's worth it as long as you don't go to a predatory school and don't drown in debt. What really matters is your LSAT and undergrad GPA. You can use this site to put in your undergrad GPA and see what different LSAT scores will get you into and how much in scholarships. It's mostly LSAT though that determines it, but there's a lot of free LSAT test prep stuff online and it is a very learnable test.
https://lsd.law/law-school-chances-predictor

62273654
Lawyers will be replaced with A.I.

62275142
>>62273654
Sort of like how your dad was replaced by my penis

62275176
>>62272812
Tits or ass

62275435
>>62272812

Are you going to be an actual trial attorney with balls or be a paper pushing cuck like most?

I can't make up my mind who's intelligence the general public overrates more between doctors and lawyers.

62275484
>>62272812
What was your LSAT score? What is your school’s rank? What was your GPA? Where are you interning this summer?

How big is your pp?

62275816
>>62275176
Ass for most of my life but as I’ve gotten older, tits
>>62275435
I honestly don’t know yet, it’s only been one year and I’m not even dead set on which area of the law I want to practice in.
>>62275484
>What was your LSAT score?
170
>What is your school’s rank?
Mid 20s
>What was your GPA?
In undergrad or 1L? In undergrad it was a 3.4 in an easy major, I just didn’t give a shit in college. In 1L it was a 3.5
>Where are you interning this summer?
I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

>How big is your pp?
5.8 inches massive

62275842
Do you ever consider yourself a second class profession compared to finance chads who value and trade and make money off the silly little financial obligation contracts lawyers write up?

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

62280365
>>62278366
You will miss out of some of the best opportunities in this profession if you don’t network, unfortunately. I only got my current job because one of my professors was willing to refer me to someone on the inside.

62280758
Are there any good legal loopholes for evading debt collection? I'm writing a story, and there's a chapter where the MC scrambles to hide his assets from the chapter 7 trustee.

62281712
>>62280758
I know several but will tell you none

item.title: 114T merger DTCC/ Stellar (XLM) — 22 replies — 21.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
114T merger DTCC/ Stellar (XLM)

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-27 22:57 UTC

OP

Today,
@The_DTCC
and SDF are announcing plans to enable the tokenization of DTC-custodied assets on Stellar.

The connection supports the rapid conversion of traditional assets into tokenized form, and the full asset lifecycle, including corporate actions and reporting.

Early replies (first 3)

62280501
Oh no no no
Chainlink sisters our response?!???

62280510
>>62280501
well this one is obviously fake/nothing burger
the chainlink announcement was the only legit one
but also they are trying to hide chainlink and keep it down

62280537
>>62280370
Stellar is part of SCALE

This just means you can bridge assets from DTCC chain to stellar using CCIP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62280553
https://chain.link/advocates
applications re-open September 2025

62280560
btw how come stellar gets a nice +10% pump but LINK gets nothing when its even bigger news?

62280655
>>62280501
Chainlink sits above all this and stellar is a SCALE partner. Were going to see more of this with chainlink connected chains.

62280835
>>62280501
> this collaboration advances our multichain strategy
Hmmmm....

62280995
>>62280370
I fucking HATE WITH MY GUTS how when ANYTHING gets a FUCKING press release like this fucking XLM shit THEY FUCKING GET TO PUMP like 10% intra day but with LINK oh nonononono WITH LINK NEWS MEAN SHIT and we don't pump even though we get 10x more institutional news than anyone. WHY FUCKING WHY IS IT LIKE THIS???????????

62281023
>>62280995
you know why
too many huwhite chuddos holding link, jewey lizard folk no likey

62281033
>>62281023
SO WHAT'S THE FUCKING POINT THEN!!!!!!!!!!!!!! We were right about EVERYTHING except for the fucking price AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA WHY DO WE NOT PUMP AND THE REST OF FUCKING NIGGER COINS ALWAYS PUMP WHEN THEY GET NEWS

62281141
>>62280501
comfy

62281194
>>62281033
That's the nature of this world it seems, bemoaning isn't gonna change anything. Those same lizards will use their unlimited funny money to make the markets do whatever they wish until they control a sizable position of link. Might explain why bitcoin does what it does why clarity gets delayed and why wallets holding 100k link has been steadily increasing during... Also just the sheer monkey faggotry of the (((market))) and these tourist faggots flooding the board shilling the dog shit fake & gay zigger hype etc.

Lizards just don't want to tip their hand and have the goyim wake up before they own it.

I'm not gonna sell.

62281212
>>62281194
And the thing is even though they are "forced" to tell us about it, doesnt mean they will make it easy

62281213
>>62281194
Fuck you I'm so tired of retarded cope and conspiracy theories about lizards and shiet. The fact is that I can't fucking understand why when CHAINLINK GETS ANNOUNCED TO GO LIVE WITH THE DTCC we fucking DUMP yet when XLM does it then ooooooooooooo of course masssa here is your +10% massa

62281222
>>62281213
bro its simple, whole market revolves around suppression of LINK

If you cant handle it sell and buy something else

62281231
>>62281213
Man I watched the fed council and thought about this shit into a framework that makes sense of it and that's good enough for me, go market sell I don't care

FUCK YOU DOUBLE NIGGER

62281241
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PPTIYk0dnXA

62281261
>>62281194
Jews need to realize that I can remain irrational and autistic longer than they can remain solvent.

62281276
>>62281222
I CAN'T FUCKING SELL BECAUSE IF I SELL AND IT PUMPS I WILL FUCKING KILL MYSELF OK????? i can't sell I'm a prisoner.

62281393
>>62281276
I fail to see the issue here.

62281696
>>62281213
BECAUSE FUCK A 10% NIGGER. WE WANT 10,000% AND THAT DOES NOT HAPPEN WITHOUT MAXIMUM PAIN AND SUFFERING.

62281838
Wait my heavy bags of 6k xlm are worth something?

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 261 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 261 • Updated: 2026-05-27 10:53 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

62269694
>>62268709
It's pretty easy if you let her use a wand or clit sucking vibrator to give herself orgasms. The only tricky part is YOU cumming during one of her 5 or 8 orgasms.

62269751
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing
Why not? Are you a woman? It's usually only parasites annoyed by this.

62269890
>incel bait (((mysteriously))) ruins another anti waging thread
this reddit poster is obviously some low test gooner soiboi. In reality 1.9m in an index fund can get you around 150k a year which is more than enough for a non-incel life especially with a paid off house.

62269901
>>62259935
I'm 37 and once I hit a 2m net worth in the next couple of years I'm going to "retire" and go back into academia. It doesn't pay very well but at least the work is enjoyable and I get to spend time with other scientists instead of these bullshit artists in industry.

62269979
>>62259935
No women? The entire point of FIRE is to fuck wives while the husband works...did you even read the manifesto?

62270348
>>62259935
I'm 40 with only ~$400k net worth. No kids/wife/never been on a date. $25k annual spend.
Just get me a rope...

62270366
How dare you??
You are just a damn child.

62270374
>>62270348
I had $400k net worth when I was 40. I'm 44 now with $1.7 million net worth. A lot can change in a short amount of time.

62270394
>>62270374
>A lot can change in a short amount of time.
Bitcoin's growth is slower now and that's my only hope.
My salary is currently $100k and I don't see potential for any significant raises in my future.
At least I think I'll have the balls to exit the system at $1M and maybe find a part time job stocking shelves to keep me busy.

62270406
>>62270348
You're not missing anything. Society is fucked. I didn't start dating until I was ~33. One woman that I dated when I was 34 ruined me forever. Not financially - just mentally.

I'm worse off financially than you because of student loans but in a few more years, I'll be in better shape albeit still with like half of your net worth when I hit your age. You're in okay shape. You're doing much better than most of your peers. You can get the validation from a woman if you push for it but it won't help. I got validation from other women after the crazy one and I didn't feel a thing. Hopefully you have nieces and nephews for whom you can leave something behind. That's my plan even though I'll probably never even meet my nieces or nephews. So it goes.

62270414
>>62270406
Thanks, bro. I've got my music, I guess.
>nieces and nephews
Nah. I'm a single child raised by a single mother. Once she's gone, I'm all alone.

62270422
>>62270414
Sorry man. My nieces and nephews are the children of my cousins, not any siblings, and I don't have a relationship with my cousins. For all intents and purposes, I'll be pretty alone when my parents are gone and they're both up there in age.

Music is my thing too. I don't enjoy it as much anymore though. Nothing hits the same after that woman. You still have a lot of time to find someone especially since you have your shit together financially but just be careful. I don't want anyone to ever have to feel the way I do on account of a woman.

62270444
>>62270422
I hope things will work out for us in the end.
I'd definitely rather be in a band (something like Jaga Jazzist) than a relationship :) We'll see were things go.

62270945
>>62262891

OnlyFans is always hiring: no experience necessary, work from home at your own hours, no dress code required

62271089
>>62259968
so dont marry. i literally do not understand why you would if you have money and a house?
horny? hire a prostitute.
lonely? get friends, or see above.
existential crisis? realize its fake and gay, we all go into the ground eventually. dead and rotting in the ground.

62271100
>>62270366
i thought the chinese have a huge surplus of men
my mom got pregnant when she was 40 and had me. i only have a touch of 'tism

62271108
>>62268693
All the FIREs are also all bogles. They do not have the brains or cojones to try to get a +3%/year outperformance on the SPY (which I get easily in one of my bad but green months). They do not trust themselves that grand feat.

62271141
>>62263178
Does your "t" button not work?

62271142
>>62261104
Tom Brady is a very swell guy. Everyone should check out his book "The TB12 Method"

62271171
>>62269901
I'm 27, any tips and tricks?

62271254
>>62260803
cant I just do this with crypto and give my mommey the sneed phrase?

62274663
Never retire, goy.

62278818
>>62262925
She's lying for attention

item.title: cardano, ADA is imploding right now — 38 replies — 19.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
cardano, ADA is imploding right now

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-05-27 22:03 UTC

OP

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

Early replies (first 3)

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62275723
>>62275591
Charles-sama, why?

62276473
>>62275591
Imagine buying cardanus, ever! This shit has less than 500$ stables liquidity onchain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62277363
>>62277151
Noooooo not the tranny support center!!!!1!1!1

62277373
>>62275591
It sucks to see a well-known coin go through such a bad situation, but that's why focusing on truly decentralized coins is the most important. Those that can't be mined in pools or via GPU/ASIC/FPGA, like Nerva/XNV, ensure everything is actually decentralized and split across the entire ecosystem.

62277405
>>62277373
> bad situation
the bro literally hard forked the ADA chain to steal unclaimed coon

62277437
>>62275698
It was always gonna end up this way. Blockchain tech is actually not that useful, some web3 use cases were kind of neat but almost all of the problems they proposed to “solve” already have better solutions that are more efficient. Combine that with the fact that “utilities” all ended up becoming speculative assets and you can’t build an economic ecosystem on shifting sands.
Crypto’s biggest appeal was being an unregulated casino where you could get away with shit that would get you arrested in regulated markets.

62277444
>>62275591
It’s about fucking time. Cardano has never had a use case and has been useless shit for almost a decade.

62277590
>>62277437
Blockchain tech is actually useful in a variety of ways, but it’s not useful to your everyday Joe or Bob. I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares? No one, that’s who.

62277632
>>62277590
Sit down boomer

62277725
>>62275591
What happened?

62278173
>>62277373
Saaar

62278448
>>62277437
>Blockchain tech is actually not that useful

https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

62278452
What kind of retard do you have to be to be interested in Cardano? Same as polkadot. Jesuschrist, it's a blockchain and they make it out to be like if it was a gothic cathedral.

Hyperliquid is less than 2 years old and is already the most used blockchain.

It was obvious it was all bullshit.

62278457
>>62278452
hyperliquid is closer to a private blockchain. it only has 27 validators.

62278477
>>62276491
This. A major kike stakeholder in a financial scam - never seen before.
In general, I'll never trust a crypto or financial project with a kike or a software project with jeets.

62278491
>>62278477
is Charles a jew? i always assumed he was a goy with all the western shit
>>62277725
i don't know, i sold all my ada in 2021.. but basically charles been scamming the treasury or something for years, makes sense, not surprised.. recently japanese validators or whatever voted to end funding the ""research"" laps funded by cardano.. charles apperently has always gone apeshit and burned every bridge but now it's become exponential bridge burnings and blockings..
ADA is a scam... nothing new.. but finally seeing Charles flame out in real time is kinda neat

62278891
>>62278491

62279915
The cope in this thread is unreal. Cry more, kiddies.

62280492
The narrative haven't really been evolving. Haven't seen your threads for some time and this is the buy signal I honestly was looking and not hoping for. Because the hate your try to project in inconsequential to you.

62280512
I'm not in ADA since 2021.
But now, i am daily scammed by BTC.
Dunno what's worse (financially) desu.

62280533
>>62277725
Some cheap twitter beef causing uproar. It's still one of the most technologically sound chains out there, but some people tried a power grab the minute Charles burnt his keys.
So the only mistake that Charles has made there is that he had too much good faith in those that are stabbing him in the back now. I guess that's what you get if you don't know who your real friends are.

62280595
jeets are still buying crypto lmao
lmfao even

62280622
>>62278448
This cope again
Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets” then maybe I’ll change my mind. Until then I think you’re delusional if you think it’s useful or “mass adoption” to have a handful of turbonerd billionaires and hedge funds use USDC or UST in place of traditional clearinghouses

62280637
>>62277444
But muh Haskell

62280714
>>62280622
>Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets”
DTCC is going to use Chainlink for stablecoins too you know.

62280809
>>62278477
It's a pretty good heuristic

62280833
Save what value you can, swap your ADA for XMR and hodl!

62281020
>>62280595
crypto is banned in india
so in a way, you're like a jeet

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608
ENJOY YOUR CARDANO, EARTHMAN!

62281639
>>62275591
>@TheCardanoTimes

62281653
>>62281159
This, one look at that basedboi faggot should tell you all you need to know

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 93 replies — 19.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-05-27 19:15 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273819
>>62271935
Ok but a lot depends on your risk tolerance and what kind of returns you’re expecting from this stage of the market.

62273824
>>62272659
>too bad you didnt buy at $200 or you'd be retired
retired on what? US dollars from selling your shitty worthless digital ponzi coin? so you admit bitcoin has no purpose or use other than hoping other retards buy it to pump up the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273959
>>62273824
So you're buying stocks in the hope some retards will pump the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273985
>>62271712
The narrative keeps shifting. First it was supposed to finally defeat the dollar, then it was supposed to be ATMs everywhere, then it's supposed to be digital gold, then the government and blackrock buy it, then it's not SUPPOSED to be in atms and then it's just really a leverage tech stock without any tech.

It's insanity. How am I supposed to know what the price will be in 10 years if the narrative cycle is like in 2?

62273994
>>62273787
This guy who spams this in every discussion has convinced me that what he's talking about is a scam and I'll never use it.

62274011
>>62271712
I have a little bitcoin, but I need large amounts of cash on hand.

62274038
>>62273994
>DYOR
>he does not DYOR
Doesn't matter that much though since you can just keep scaling by putting gains into new assets rather than ever staking.

62274120
Let's buy more BTC

62274282
>>62273959
yes beause stocks represnt ownership in a company which is a real asset with actual cashflow unlike a digital ponzi based on a generic internet token with zero utility that anyone can make at any time

also im not pretending stocks are the future currency of the world that everyone is going to use like the retard dipshits such as yourself

62274315
>>62273655
>i dont care if it goes to 1million
the /biz/ way

62274703
>>62273994
You didn’t even look into how it works and already calling it a scam lol. At least research it first before getting emotional.

62274705
>>62271712
It needs to go down to $50, so I will buy them.

otherwise GTFO!

62274708
>>62271960
Most people only believe in BTC after the price already moves far beyond what they once thought was impossible.

62275419
>>62271712
I am always buying BTC.

62275451
I don't have tens of thousands of dollars to blow on just one type of investment. Sorry I noticed too late and was too financially illiterate to trust it over a decade ago.

62275467
>>62271712
>make gains elsewhere
>rotate into BTC
That's how I have to go about it being such a latecomer.

62275501
>>62271712
returns are too low at this point, and it can go to 0 at any time. why are you holding bags?

62276869
>>62272002
BTC doesn’t need to become the global reserve currency overnight to justify current valuations. You people still think adoption is binary for some reason.

62276880
>>62272017
CLARITY isn't going to happen, and the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve won't mean shit unless it is passed as a bill in Congress (which also won't happen)

62276888
Fear
I actually think crypto seems retarded. I also don't understand what makes Bitcoin any different than the 10,000 other shitcoins out there. Yeah Bitcoin was first, but that is the only think I can understand that makes it special compared to numerous other coins.

I feel extreme regret for not buying it. I just make myself feel better by telling myself I would have sold it when I hit my first 5x and I never would have been able to hodl this long.

I literally almost bought $100 of Bitcoin when it was like $5 each. I closed the window at the last moment because I thought it was stupid. It feels like throwing away a winning lottery card whenever I think about it.

62278410
>>62276888
You just didnt make money on crypto

62278419
>>62276880
Retard. Stop smoking

62278837
>>62274703
How you can say it's legit?

62278840
>>62275501
can go to zero? In what way

62278849
>>62274705
Delusional joker

62278858
>>62273985
It had always been fishy.

62280044
>>62278837
You can literally verify everything yourself. We’re way past the point where major players like Aave, Ledger, GoMining, a16z and others would touch obvious garbage for no reason.

62280052
>>62272017
A lot of people still view the market through old cycle assumptions.

62280441
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a liquidity sink and will be rugged in a bug deflatinary swoop when the time is right.

62280858
>>62273653
problem with index funds is you just never make enough to make it unless you're like 20-30 years at it

I am in tech with wfh. I am the top 10%. I have NO job security, not anymore. The top 10% will become the top 5% soon. T…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-27 23:26 UTC

OP

I am in tech with wfh. I am the top 10%. I have NO job security, not anymore. The top 10% will become the top 5% soon. Then 2%. Then 1%. Then a handful.

Majority of people are even more screwed. Something has to be done.

62281758
Buy a pressure washer

62281770
>>62281751

62281771

62281871
>>62281770

item.title: /Biz revival — 127 replies — 9.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 127 • Updated: 2026-05-27 11:10 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271377
>>62265877
I don't think there's a LUNC thread up now but I remember seeing one recently where there was a lot of reasonable optimism.

62271378
>>62265467
It's dead Jim

62271387
>>62270867
What's the basis for uranium at this time? I saw a few months ago people talking about various reactors, from new mini ones to restarting some existing ones.

62271407
>>62271387
an anon shilled some us stock, nano nuclear energy and i didn't buy because the chart looked already pumped. it went down a lot since like 40%

Nano nuclear energy. I looked it up they're trying to develop mini nuclear reactors thant can be moved around, like for data centers etc. But i'm wondering isn't that tech super risky?in any case that might or might not be a great moonshot. No idea, don't own any but looking at it

62271421
>>62271407
I can see what you would be. I remember seeing some airplane-lifted reactors some months ago. Operation Windlord. Valar Atomics, though, they are not publicly traded.
I see what you mean about NNE having doing about a 10x since it began and now being like 50% down since then. Still, nice to have specific leads on what to research.

62271501
>>62265467
Its dead because there havent been enough nigger days due to the bull market

62271653
How about starting with removing this faggot nigger captcha and having to wait for a minute for it? I gave up posting here, because of it, honestly. And I'm not verifying my email each time either.

62271675
>>62271653
Captchas become faster and easier if you post more

62271760
>>62271675
and then reset your progress for no reason
no, I didn't erase my cookies, just couldn't post for TWO DAYS

62271767
>>62271760
yes jannies trannies destroyed it all. it's over. At least biz is the only white board left

62271790
>>62265467
good pick with litecoin anon, literally the only coin which can reliably process frequent micropayments without clogging or taking too many fees


it will be the coin of choice for autonomous AI agents to transact with eachother in the future

62271899
>>62265467
what killed this place to begin with is that there is no business or finance talk going on here. Its just retarded "buy this crypto coin!!!"

62272124
>>62265467
>LTC
Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.

>TON
Shit is never going to pump.

>UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.
>IF
They won't ever back down their bullshit, even in the deepest shit they still prefered to appeal to niggers and fags instead of making games.

62272342
>>62272124
>Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.
they just developped MWEB transactions anon. Try harder.

why wouldn't ton pump? are you retarded?

nvgmi

62272366
>>62265467
india and china need to be banned from this board

62273281
>>62271298
Seems like it crashes X times more too unfortunately. Maybe a good idea for ltc exposure but I’m really unsure if litecoin will be the play next bull

62273290
>>62265467
>we need to revive biz
>how do we do it you ask
>well its simple you just buy my decade old bags of a shitcoin so i can finally break even
well guess we found aid devastated bald whiny fag and its op

62274410
>>62269707
Yeah I think Litecoin "trojan horses" privacy into mainstream cryptocurrency. The marketing around it has always been "OG coin".
Eventually the markets will realize privacy is useful for more than cipherpunkery, and is embraced by big business.
Related, there was a lot of ZK research done by Polygon teams, but their funding ran out, I think? There's some other smaller projects experimenting with very compact proofs, Mina Protocol I think. I haven't researched that much. I'm a fan of LTC for being OG - strong codebase.

62274471
>>62273281
crashes x times more

not really. right now the price of the stock is like a 20% discount over their litecoin treasury. it gets giga hyped if litecoin moons. Now if litecoin were to moon for real one day (let's say 800$) this stock would probably x 50 from where it is.
The thing i don't like about LITS is basically not your key, not your cryptos. So i'm 30% LITS 70% litecoin

>>62274410
it's really a revolution. I think btc will implement the same thing. It's an integrated coin mixer that can't be traced basically.

62275749
>>62272342
few people know that aside from the privacy aspect, mweb also makes the network scalable because of pruning

without pruning a blockchain ends up taking infinite storage space

62276184
>>62274410
>>62274471
This leading to btc implementing something similar would be insane. XMR will likely always be the king but some more mainstream privacy adoption will be a net positive I think

62276504
>>62275749
didn't know that. I guess so far blockchain size wasn't really an issue because it's not so big and you can run pruned nodes already? maybe i was mistaken.

>>62276184
now that i think about it, i'm 100% positive this is going to happen. Privacy is a huge issue for crypto, and a huge positive when it is implemented. Too many home jacking, fraud, taxes, you avoid all this shit. Bitcoin coin mixers were a weak option to try to anonymize btc. Mweb basically wash any coin in and out and will be as hard to trace as monero. Hopefully litecoin gets to shine because of it before btc implements it.

62276517
>>62276504

62276943
>>62276517
BALDY BALDY OVER THERE,
WHAT’S IT LIKE TO HAVE NO HAIR?
IS IT HOT OR IS IT COLD?
I DON’T KNOW ‘CAUSE I'M NOT BALD!

62276952
>>62276943
Knew it was you

62276981
>>62276952
the id change itself for no reason sometimes idk why

62276989
>>62276981
Sure sure....

62277056
>>62265475
>Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
Scam Altman

62278383
>>62276504

with bitcoin or xmr you can run a pruned node to save on space, but you still need a full unpruned node to serve you the initial chain data

in MW, including the mweb chain litecoin has, the chain itself is compressed, there is no pruned vs unpruned node here

storage was never problematic because these networks never had full throughput usage for extended period of time but with increasingly more autonomous ai systems that could change

62278851
>>62265467
Most of my alts are deep in the red. Tron has been the most stable. Bitcoin is over 90% of my crypto portfolio thank god. My strategy in investing combines /pmg/ /smg/ and buying BTC.

62274585

62274618
>>62272398
Finally some good new content

62276307
Job Posting: Goy Cattle (Entry-Level Herd)
Company: Shekelberg Global Asset ManagementLocation: The Real World™ (No Remote)
Salary: $17k + participation trophies
Type: Lifelong Indenture

About Us
We manage the money. You manage the reality. Perfect partnership.

Position Summary
Hiring motivated Goy Cattle to handle all the physical, taxable, everyday bullshit while we short futures from the penthouse.

Key Responsibilities
• Live in the Real World™ daily: traffic, kneeling, $12 eggs, and pointless cannon fodder.

• Consume goyslop, pay taxes, and reproduce below replacement rate.
• Rage on social media about culture wars while we tweak interest rates.
• Vote blue/red (both approved), stay outraged on schedule, then return to Nigflix for goyslop and real world programming.
• Die quietly after 40 years without noticing patterns.

Qualifications
• High school diploma, (college debt a plus).
• Zero pattern recognition and foreskin.
• Loves onions, forever wars, and believing the news.
• Must identify as goy. Self-ID accepted.
What We Offer
• Inflation that always wins.
• Health insurance tax (claimable if brown and jobless only).
• 1vacation days + daily slavery and holocaust infotainment .
• Premium distractions: sportsball, OnlyFans, and election theater.

Equal Opportunity
We hire all goyim — White, Black, Brown, etc. The more divided, the easier to manage.

How to ApplyEmail résumé + soul to goyim@shekelberg.globalSubject: “Ready to Graze.”

62277367
>>62276307

62279772
>>62276307

Wait to they want college grads on purpose because they have to pay back student loans and need to service their debt. Sounds a lot like having a job and buying a new car. You can’t quit because you are a literal debt slave.

62279800
>>62274245

Maybe I should apply

62279820
>>62271605

>>62272133
